analysis.reasoning: Core message: An aphorism about resilience—after loss, many continue; after sickness, many thrive—followed by optimism that the world will emerge stronger after COVID-19. Assessment by narrative: CH1 (West immoral/hostile) – not referenced; no criticism of the West. CH2 (China as benevolent power) – no mention of China’s international role, cooperation, or multilateralism. CH3 (China’s epic history) – no historical or CCP-related resilience specific to China; the message is universal. CH4 (appropriate political system/values) – no advocacy of China’s political system or values. CH5 (CCP government succeeds) – no claims about China’s governance or achievements. CH6 (China’s culture/nature/heritage) – although the opening quote has a proverbial tone, the tweet doesn’t promote Chinese culture or heritage explicitly. Conclusion: The tweet promotes a general hopeful sentiment about the pandemic, not any listed China-specific narrative.
